title: The One Where Ross And Rachel Take A Bre

jokes.text: Hey, how much you give me to eat this whole jar of olives? / I won't give you anything, but you'll owe me 2.95.
jokes.setup: Joey asks for payment to eat olives, implying he'd need incentive
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out Joey would have to pay for the olives himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey food obsession meets Chandler's dry wit. Well-structured with perfect economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: Done.
jokes.setup: Being told he'd owe money for the olives
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately agrees anyway, showing his food priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - one word that reveals his complete lack of financial priorities when food is involved.

jokes.text: I have a date with this diplomat I met while I was giving free massages outside the U.N.
jokes.setup: Normal request for an atlas for a date
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd circumstances of meeting someone at the U.N. while giving massages
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Phoebe - takes a mundane situation and makes it wonderfully absurd through her unique worldview.

jokes.text: Oh, it's my new thing. I figure, bodies at peace make peace.
jokes.setup: Someone questioning her U.N. massage scheme
jokes.punchline: Her pseudo-profound justification with wordplay
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe pseudo-wisdom with a peace/piece wordplay that sounds meaningful but is completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: Wow. You might just get the first Nobel Prize in rubbing.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's peace-through-massage philosophy
jokes.punchline: Mock-elevated description of massage as Nobel-worthy
jokes.explanation: Solid pun playing on Nobel Peace Prize, though 'rubbing' makes it slightly crude. Good structure but not highly original.

jokes.text: [SPEAKING INDISTINCTLY] / There's a G in there.
jokes.setup: Asked what country her diplomat date is from
jokes.punchline: Can't pronounce the country name, only identifies one letter
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy of mispronunciation. Works for Phoebe's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Where's that? / In your atlas. / I don't have an atlas. / Oh. / Oh, but wait, I do have a globe. Hold on.
jokes.setup: Looking for a country location
jokes.punchline: Series of back-and-forth revelations about available geography tools
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion comedy but drags on too long for the payoff. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: What is this? / It's a globe. / And a pencil sharpener.
jokes.setup: Phoebe bringing out her globe to help
jokes.punchline: Globe is comically small/inappropriate, doubles as pencil sharpener
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that's both absurd and practical. The combination is unexpected and perfectly Phoebe.

jokes.text: Okay, listen, just give me anything I can make two of.
jokes.setup: Joey offering to copy things at Xerox place
jokes.punchline: Reveals his real motive is just to see the hot girl
jokes.explanation: Joey's transparent attempt to justify his crush. Character-appropriate but predictable setup.

jokes.text: If you don't have anything to copy, why are you going there? / Yeah, are you just going down there to gawk at that hot girl with the bellybutton ring again?
jokes.setup: Joey's weak excuse for going to copy place
jokes.punchline: Direct callout of his obvious motive with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Standard expose-the-obvious-motive joke. The belly button ring detail adds some specificity but overall predictable.

jokes.text: Chloe, switch with me. There are some guys here who got a crush on you. / Okay, that hurt us.
jokes.setup: Copy shop employee announcing Joey and Chandler's crush
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan reaction to being outed so bluntly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler understatement in response to embarrassment. Economy of language and character-specific reaction.

jokes.text: Hi, guys. I haven't seen you since this morning. / Well, uh... / You know.
jokes.setup: Chloe casually mentioning seeing them earlier
jokes.punchline: Joey's awkward non-response revealing he's been stalking
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe comedy as Joey's stalking behavior is exposed. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, what are you guys doing tomorrow night? / Both of us? / Maybe. Does that scare you?
jokes.setup: Chloe asking both guys to an event
jokes.punchline: Joey's eager/nervous response to potential threesome implications
jokes.explanation: Joey's typical over-eager response to sexual possibilities. In character but relies on obvious horndog stereotype.

jokes.text: Aw. Me too. Although this city is my home. So that's dumb, what I said. Don't tell him I said that.
jokes.setup: Romantic comment about city feeling like home
jokes.punchline: Phoebe realizes the logical flaw in her response and awkwardly backpedals
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe moment where her honesty creates awkwardness. Good self-awareness and character consistency.

jokes.text: Okay. You know what? You don't have to do that now. / No, no, not him. No, you don't.
jokes.setup: Translator saying diplomat wants to kiss Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's panicked response and confusion about who should do what
jokes.explanation: Basic misunderstanding comedy around the three-person dynamic. Works but isn't particularly inspired.

jokes.text: Wow, it's small. / Yeah, but Sergei said it took the Germans six weeks to get all the way across it.
jokes.setup: Looking at small country on globe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's illogical historical reference that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic from Phoebe. The non-sequitur historical reference is perfectly illogical yet delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: What's that on the bottom? / Oh. That's my doodle of a ladybug with a top hat. Ha, ha. / She's fancy.
jokes.setup: Ross looking at Monica's phone message
jokes.punchline: Monica's elaborate doodle and proud description of fancy ladybug
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Monica's quirky artistic side. The 'she's fancy' delivery adds charm.

jokes.text: 'Kind of cute' like, 'really kind of cute'? Or 'kind of cute' like your friend Spackle-Back Larry? / Hey, don't call him that. His name is Spackle-Back Harry.
jokes.setup: Monica making fun of Phoebe's friend's name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe corrects only the first name, not the ridiculous nickname
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Sets up expectation that Phoebe will defend the insulting nickname, but she only corrects the first name. Perfect Phoebe logic.

jokes.text: Ha, ha. Dude, I don't know. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what they'd do in a threesome scenario
jokes.punchline: Joey's nervous laughter revealing his anxiety about the situation
jokes.explanation: Basic nervous reaction. The forced laughter conveys awkwardness but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Never open your eyes. You know? Because you don't want to be doing something and then look up, right? And see something that you don't want to be seeing.
jokes.setup: Establishing threesome ground rules
jokes.punchline: Joey's graphic concern about visual contact during the act
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy about the practical concerns of a threesome. A bit verbose but captures Joey's anxious oversharing.

jokes.text: What if my eyes are closed, and my hand is out there... / Aah! Okay, eyes open at all times.
jokes.setup: Joey continuing threesome logistics
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified realization about accidental contact leading to rule reversal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from previous rule. Chandler's horror and immediate rule change is well-timed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, if you don't know that, then I don't want to do this with you.
jokes.setup: Joey asking what's heads and tails in coin flip context
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock-serious rejection based on sexual inexperience
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that both advances the sexual comedy and shows Chandler's quick wit. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: My God. What are you doing here? / Well, you said you couldn't go out, so... / You brought a picnic. Ugh. What a boyfriend. That's it. On Monday, I start wearing makeup.
jokes.setup: Ross surprising Rachel at work with romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Coworker Sophie's self-deprecating reaction about needing to up her game
jokes.explanation: Nice observational humor about relationship comparison. Sophie's makeup line is a relatable self-improvement reaction.

jokes.text: Heh. Pepper? / None for me. / Okay, sorry. Whoo.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to be romantic during Rachel's work crisis
jokes.punchline: Awkward pepper offer during inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor about romantic timing. The pepper offer shows how out of touch Ross is with the situation.

jokes.text: Excuse me, I'm sorry, I'm gonna have to call you back. I've got Shemp in my office.
jokes.setup: Ross making a mess during Rachel's important call
jokes.punchline: Rachel referring to Ross as Three Stooges character
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that fits the slapstick situation. Shemp is the right choice for bumbling behavior.

jokes.text: You do not bring a picnic basket to somebody's work unless maybe they were a park ranger.
jokes.setup: Argument about Ross bringing picnic to office
jokes.punchline: Rachel's specific exception for park rangers
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor with specific, logical exception. The park ranger detail makes it quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: The X-5000 makes an X-50 look like a T-71.
jokes.setup: Chloe explaining copy machine technology
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical comparison using made-up model numbers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about tech specs. The illogical comparison sounds technical but means nothing.

jokes.text: And now any time anybody wants 400, we just say, 'Let's Ross it.'
jokes.setup: Story about Ross demanding 400% enlargement
jokes.punchline: His name became workplace slang for excessive requests
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing how Ross's intensity created workplace mythology. 'Let's Ross it' is highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, you just asked if I wanted to go to bed with you tonight. / Oh, my God. / No wonder I get such great service at Café Maurice.
jokes.setup: Monica accidentally propositioning with her French
jokes.punchline: Realization about why restaurant service has been so good
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes past experiences. The revelation is both funny and mortifying for Monica.

jokes.text: So you're a chef? / I'm also thinking of opening up my own restaurant. / Oh, really? / Monica... could I talk to you behind my menu, please?
jokes.setup: Translator flirting with Monica
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's desperate attempt at privacy using menu as shield
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with menu as inadequate privacy shield. Shows Phoebe's desperation about her date being hijacked.

jokes.text: Why? What happened to him? / Nothing. He just really believes in that.
jokes.setup: Swimming-after-eating myth discussion leading to Uncle Lenny reference
jokes.punchline: Anticlimax - uncle is fine, just gullible about the myth
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection. Sets up expectation of tragedy, delivers harmless but character-revealing anticlimax instead.

jokes.text: I told him he treats me like a park ranger, something, ugh...
jokes.setup: Rachel recounting her fight with Ross
jokes.punchline: Callback to her earlier park ranger comment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Rachel used her earlier joke in the actual fight. Adds consistency to her character voice.

jokes.text: I just hope you don't accidentally suck it up through your nose and choke on it.
jokes.setup: Giving tissue to crying translator
jokes.punchline: Monica's inappropriate concern about tissue-related death
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica over-concern about safety. Dark but character-appropriate worry about mundane tissue use.

jokes.text: You silly diplomat. Why don't you learn some English, Sergei?
jokes.setup: Monica frustrated with translation situation
jokes.punchline: Her direct, culturally insensitive demand
jokes.explanation: Monica's blunt frustration creates cringe humor. Shows her lack of diplomatic social skills despite good intentions.

jokes.text: My guy has coupons. / Your guy can't even say 'coupons.'
jokes.setup: Monica bragging about translator having diplomatic coupons
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sharp comeback about language barrier
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with excellent economy. Phoebe cuts through Monica's bragging with simple logic about pronunciation.

jokes.text: See, we don't need them. / Plate? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Sergei trying to communicate without translator
jokes.punchline: Simple one-word communication actually working
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing communication transcends language. Simple but effective for character development.

jokes.text: You're not home.
jokes.setup: Ross desperately trying to reach Rachel
jokes.punchline: His obvious statement to empty apartment
jokes.explanation: Simple understatement showing Ross's desperation. Economy works but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: American. You know, it's a very hard language.
jokes.setup: Teaching Sergei English through 'American Pie' song
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sincere assessment of English difficulty
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about language learning. Fits Phoebe's earnest but slightly off-kilter worldview.
